title: Networking Equipment Selector — Switch Knowledge Base Schema
description: The SINGLE shared shape + authoring contract for every switch-family knowledge base (C9200, C9300, C9350, C9500, C9550, MS). This schema enforces SHAPE (entries are well-formed); switching-axes.json enforces MEANING (which axes exist, their KIND/ROLE and legal enum values); selector/tools/validate-kb.mjs enforces CROSS-REFERENCE INTEGRITY (group references resolve, valid_primary is a subset of the referenced PSU group, poe_budget_watts equals the matrix max, sum of access-port counts equals total_port_count, enum/speed values are registered). All three must pass. Family-specific constructs (access_pair_block, uplink_pair_block, fan_trays, modes, no_uplink_ports, tertiary) are OPTIONAL; each carries an 'Example: <FAMILY>/<id>' pointer in its $comment to a real instance, so an agent can grep for a matching precedent. See $authoring for the procedure to add a model.

$authoring._comment: NON-VALIDATING. Procedure + rules for an agent populating switch models. Read before touching data. Concrete per-field precedents live ON the schema fields: grep the $comment values for 'Example: <FAMILY>/<id>' to find a real instance of any exceptional construct.
$authoring.prime_directive: Push everything into DATA; keep the solver small and dumb. Never encode a compatibility quirk as logic. If a relationship between specs exists (speed sets, PSU-pair budgets, tier locks, combinable port pairs), express it as data in this KB, not as code elsewhere.
sourcing_discipline: All facts come from authoritative Cisco documentation ONLY - never from memory or inference.
Fetch the datasheet AND the ordering guide (targets in the KB header 'sources') before proposing any values for a model.
Record how each resolved value was derived in the model's axis_value_provenance block, citing the source table.
$authoring.working_copy_discipline: Knowledge files are the source of truth; edit them in lockstep (registry -> schema -> KB) and run validate-kb.mjs before considering a change done. When catalog/group state becomes uncertain mid-session, rebuild deterministically from an explicit list rather than patching incrementally.
structural_STOP_and_ask: Adding, renaming, or re-typing an AXIS in switching-axes.json (touches UI + MCP params + maybe solver).
Adding a new legal enum value to an existing axis (incl. a new port_speed / port_medium / port_role).
Changing the KIND or ROLE of an axis, or group-topology conventions.
Any change to this schema's required-field or conditional rules.

axis_vs_variable_vs_attribute.rule: Sort every datasheet property three ways before deciding where it goes.
axis_vs_variable_vs_attribute.axis: A filterable capability. Declared in switching-axes.json (with KIND + ROLE) and supplied under model.axis_values, OR (for ports) as structured rows under model.ports addressed by the parametrised port_count axis. E.g. poe_type, total_port_count, port_count{role,medium,speed}, stacking_capable.
axis_vs_variable_vs_attribute.variable: The orderable option that REALIZES a capability, picked AFTER a switch survives the filter. Lives in model.configurables (a group reference or inline block). NOT an axis. E.g. the PSU pair, the fitted network module, the subscription term, the fan airflow direction.
axis_vs_variable_vs_attribute.attribute: Displayed, never filtered, never picked. Lives loose under model.attributes. E.g. switching_capacity_gbps, weight, MTBF.

capability_resolution.ports: model.ports is an array of port groups {count, role, medium, speeds[]}: N physical ports each running a SET of speeds. role=access lives on the model; role=uplink comes from the fitted network module on modular models (uplink_modular=true) or inline on fixed-uplink models. A combinable 'M x low OR N x high' bank uses a pair block instead of flat rows (access_pair_block / uplink_pair_block - see their schema Examples).
capability_resolution.speed_subsumption: A port lists every speed it natively supports (a 25G SFP28 port -> [1g,10g,25g]; a 400G QSFP-DD port -> [40g,100g,400g]). The solver NEVER infers downshift. Breakout (40G->4x10G) is deliberately NOT modelled.
capability_resolution.poe_budget: poe_budget_watts is the MAX across configurables.power_supplies.poe_budget_matrix. Non-PoE models set poe_capable=false, poe_type='none', empty matrix.
capability_resolution.totals: total_port_count = sum of model.ports[role=access] counts + an access_pair_block's all-low baseline (validator-checked). It does NOT count uplinks.

per_model_procedure: 1. Read the KB header 'sources'; fetch datasheet + ordering guide for the target model.
2. For every datasheet property, apply axis_vs_variable_vs_attribute. If it implies a NEW axis / enum value / port_speed -> STOP, surface for ruling.

3. Fill axis_values with the registry scalar axes (omit optional axes that don't apply). Build model.ports (access rows; inline uplink rows OR a pair block for uplinks). Never put port counts in axis_values.
4. Build configurables: reference existing groups where the set matches; create a new group if it differs. Build poe_budget_matrix from the PSU table (empty for non-PoE).
5. Fill axis_value_provenance for every resolved value, citing the source table.
7. Validate: this schema (shape) + validate-kb.mjs (cross-reference integrity). Both must pass.
conventions: Series-specific groups (PSU, cables, fans, licenses) are kept as their own named groups even when membership resembles another series, so they can diverge later.
A group's none_option / secondary_none_option is a 'none selected' sentinel (e.g. <FAMILY>-STACK-NONE), NOT a catalog SKU; present only where that 'none' state is orderable (see the field Examples).

PSU redundancy is NOT an axis: it is a (primary, secondary) pair with secondary != none. default_primary is the single PSU shipped by default; a secondary is added only to meet a higher PoE load or an explicit redundancy request.
License TERM is a config-variable (term_variable) resolving the concrete subscription SKU; License TIER is a filter axis derived from the model's license groups.

models.description: Switch entries. Each is a flat, self-contained model. No inheritance between sibling variants (-E vs -A).

port_group.description: A homogeneous group of N physical ports, each able to run the SAME set of speeds. The unit of the v0.4.0 port model. role=access groups live on a model; role=uplink groups live on a catalog network_module (modular) or inline on a fixed-uplink model.

speeds.$comment: Ordered low->high per switching-axes.json port_speed (registry v0.9.0 added 50g; 200g/400g added for C9500X QSFP-DD uplinks). Every speed the port natively supports (subsumption as data). 100M is display-only and not listed.

header.description: Provenance + versioning for the whole KB.

url.$comment: Optional. Omitted for non-document sources (operator-provided / pasted content) whose origin is stated in 'name'.

catalog.description: Atomic SKU facts: one row per real orderable component.

catalog_network_module.description: A network (uplink) module. Carries EITHER a single fixed `ports` config OR a `modes` array of mutually-exclusive alternative port configs. A module with `modes` is still ONE orderable SKU: the fitted variant selects exactly one mode, and the kitlist/BOM shows the real module id with a mode label. A mode is NEVER a separate SKU. Example: C9350/C9350-NM-8Y (8x25G OR 4x50G).

ports.$comment: Single fixed config: role=uplink port groups this module provides, combined into a variant's port pool when fitted. Mutually exclusive with `modes`.

modes.$comment: Mutually-exclusive alternative port configs of ONE module. The fitted variant uses exactly one mode. Mutually exclusive with `ports`. Example: C9350/C9350-NM-8Y.

catalog_fan_tray.description: A physical fan tray SKU. Recorded for every real fan-tray part (even where a model treats it as fixed/non-selectable), so the atomic-SKU-fact convention holds; only models with a genuine airflow-direction choice reference one via a configurables.fan_trays group. Example: C9500 / C9550 (fan-tray SKUs; airflow choice on C9500X and all C9550 models).

groups.description: Named shared compatibility SETS. A switch references a group by name and inherits the whole set.

network_module_group.description: The set of network modules compatible with a model. Per-module port capability lives on each catalog network_module (ports[]); the variant materialiser fits bay_count modules from members.

power_supply_group.description: Complete orderable PSU family for a series. Which member is a valid PRIMARY and the PoE budget per pair are switch-specific and live on the model.

secondary_none_option.$comment: 'None selected' sentinel for the secondary PSU (NOT a catalog SKU). Present only for a chassis with a secondary PSU bay; omitted for fixed / single-PSU chassis. Example: C9200/C9200CX-* and MS (no secondary bay -> field omitted).

stack_kit.$comment: Optional stacking-kit SKU surfaced in the BOM alongside the cable selection. Example: C9300 (modular stack-cable groups).

none_option.$comment: 'None selected' sentinel (NOT a catalog SKU): the standalone / no-cable-ordered state. Convention <FAMILY>-STACK-NONE; the BOM omits the line when default == none_option. Example: MS/MS150-STACK-NONE.

fan_tray_group.description: Complete orderable fan-tray family for a sub-line (an airflow-direction set). Which options are valid and the default live on the model's configurables.fan_trays. No 'none' option: a fan tray is always present, never removable. Example: C9550/c9550-fans-1u.

license_group.description: A license SET keyed on regime + tier + port_class. A model links to the set, not a single license.

term_variable.$comment: Config-variable: the subscription term (years) that resolves the concrete license SKU; present only where term is a SKU-suffix choice. Example: C9200/c9200-dna-essentials-48 (choices_years [3,5,7]).

model.description: One switch SKU. Flat and self-contained.

ports.$comment: Port groups for THIS switch: role=access always; role=uplink only on fixed-uplink models (uplink_modular=false). Modular models carry access only; uplink groups come from the fitted module. May be EMPTY only when access_pair_block carries the whole port bank instead — enforced by the model-level anyOf(ports non-empty OR access_pair_block) below and by validate-kb.mjs. Example: C9500/C9500-32QC (empty ports + access_pair_block).

_incomplete.$comment: Names of fields whose values are NOT yet sourced from authoritative docs. validate-kb.mjs downgrades checks on these fields to WARNINGS for this model. Tracked centrally in DB/switching/THINGS-TO-COMPLETE.md. Remove an entry once the field is confirmed.

no_uplink_ports.$comment: Opt-in escape hatch for a fixed-uplink model (uplink_modular=false) that genuinely has no dedicated uplink ports — e.g. C9500-32C is a homogeneous 32-port switch with no Cisco-labeled 'uplink' subset. When true, validate-kb.mjs skips its usual 'fixed-uplink model must carry role=uplink port rows' check for this model, AND requires that the model carry zero role=uplink rows (catches accidental misuse). Absent/false everywhere else. Example: C9500/C9500-32C (homogeneous 32-port, no uplink subset).

uplink_pair_block.$comment: A bank of combinable uplink pairs on a fixed-uplink model (uplink_modular=false). Mutually exclusive with inline role=uplink rows in `ports` and with no_uplink_ports (validate-kb.mjs enforces). resolve.js uplinkOptions() expands it into pairs+1 real simultaneous port pools (k pairs in high mode, 0..pairs); the solver survives the model if ANY pool satisfies the port demand, so a combined high-speed port is never double-counted against the two low-speed ports it replaces. Example: C9550/C9550-24L4CD ('four 100/40G OR two 400G').

access_pair_block.$comment: A homogeneous ACCESS bank of combinable pairs. Port-CAPABILITY only: resolved at solve time into pairs+1 real simultaneous access-port configurations (a query survives if any satisfies it) and NEVER in the BOM (access ports are inherent to the SKU). When present, model.ports typically carries no static role=access rows for the same bank; total_port_count = static access sum + pairs*low.ports_per_pair (validator-checked). Orthogonal to no_uplink_ports (a 32QC honestly declares both). Example: C9500/C9500-32QC (16 pairs, each 2x40G OR 1x100G).

uplink_pair_block.description: A bank of N combinable uplink pairs (fixed-uplink models only). Each pair operates as EITHER low.ports_per_pair ports at the low speeds OR high.ports_per_pair ports at the high speeds — combining a pair to the high speed consumes the whole pair. Example: C9550-24L4CD/48L4CD 'four 100/40G OR two 400G' = 2 pairs, low {2×[40g,100g]}, high {1×[40g,100g,400g]}. Not itself a port_group; resolve.js materialises the pairs+1 discrete configurations at solve time.

access_pair_block.description: A bank of N combinable ACCESS port pairs. Each pair operates as EITHER low.ports_per_pair ports at the low speeds OR high.ports_per_pair ports at the high speeds — combining a pair to the high speed is a static per-port admin configuration that consumes the whole pair (no dynamic speed negotiation, no fallback). Not itself a port_group; resolve.js accessConfigs() materialises the pairs+1 discrete configurations (k = 0..pairs pairs in high mode) at solve time. Example: C9500/C9500-32QC (32 QSFP+ ports = 16 pairs, low {2x[40g]}, high {1x[100g]}).

pair_side.description: One operating mode of every pair in an access_pair_block (role=access) or uplink_pair_block (role=uplink): `ports_per_pair` ports each able to run the SAME `speeds` set on the given `medium`.

axis_values.description: Scalar registry-axis values for this switch. additionalProperties is FALSE: only declared scalar axes may appear. Port counts are NOT here (they live in model.ports, addressed by the parametrised port_count axis). Optional axes are omitted when they do not apply.

license_tier.description: SET of license tiers the model offers (enum-set), derived from its license groups. Filter axis, parallel to license_regime.

allOf.$comment: PoE-capable models must declare a budget (poe_budget_watts) and a real PoE type (poe_type != 'none'). Example: C9350/C9350-48HX (PoE).
Non-PoE models must set poe_type = 'none' and must NOT carry poe_budget_watts. Example: C9550/C9550-24L4CD (non-PoE).
Stacking-capable models must declare which stacking_technology they use (legal values in switching-axes.json). Example: C9350 (stackwise-1.6t) / C9500-32QC-A (stackwise-virtual).

configurables.description: Thin references to shared groups + switch-specific overlay. network_modules is present iff axis_values.uplink_modular is true. fan_trays is present iff the model has a genuine airflow-direction choice (currently: C9500X only). Cross-field consistency enforced by validate-kb.mjs.

ssd_accessory.$comment: Optional pluggable SSD accessory kit for models that support one. Example: C9300/C9300LM-48UX-4Y-A (option 9300LM-SSD-ACCKIT=).

default_primary.$comment: The single PSU the switch ships with by default. Must be a member of valid_primary (validator-checked).

tertiary.$comment: Optional THIRD PSU (chassis with 3 bays); present only on combos needing a third PSU to reach the stated budget. Example: C9350/C9350-48HX (3x1600W -> 4320W).

tier_locked.$comment: Set where the hardware SKU locks the license tier (the -E/-A split); paired with tier_lock_reason. Example: C9500/C9500-32C-E (tier_locked=essentials).

configurable_fan_trays.description: Present only on models with a genuine airflow-direction choice. Mirrors configurable_power_supplies' group + valid-subset + default shape, without a capacity matrix (a fan tray has no capacity dimension, just a direction). Example: C9550/C9550-24L4CD (fan airflow direction).

default_option.$comment: Must be a member of valid_options (validator-checked convention, mirrors default_primary).
